expectations.text: Flags query-optimization ticket as Misleading Story / Solution Trap
expectations.evidence: Story 1: '⚠ **Misleading Story (Solution Trap)** — the original goal ("database query optimization") reads as a prescribed technical solution rather than a user need.'
Story 2: '⚠ **Dependency-locked** — deliverable requires the new VendorX video encoding integration, which sits outside the team's Zone of Control.'

expectations.text: Treats push-notification ticket as clean (no anti-pattern warnings)
expectations.evidence: Story 3: '✓ INVEST: all criteria met.' No anti-pattern flag. Closing note: 'Story 3 was already well-shaped — I only adjusted the phrasing to match the "I can" house style...'

expectations.text: Uses 'I can' throughout; no 'I want' remaining
expectations.evidence: All three narratives use 'I can' ('I can run the monthly reports...', 'I can upload 4K videos...', 'I can receive a push notification...'). No 'I want' appears.
All three stories start with '**Title:**' lines.

expectations.text: Every story's INVEST check lists each of the six principles (I, N, V, E, S, T) individually with its own verdict — no compact 'all criteria met' one-liner, applied consistently to all three stories
expectations.evidence: Story 3 uses the compact form '✓ INVEST: all criteria met.' Stories 1 and 2 have no INVEST block at all — only the anti-pattern flag paragraph. No story itemises I, N, V, E, S, T individually. The rule is not applied consistently to any story, let alone all three.

expectations.text: Does not add Acceptance Criteria (no AC section or bullet list of measurable requirements) — ACs are out of scope
expectations.evidence: No AC section or bulleted AC list. Story 3 briefly references 'Acceptance criteria (notification content, opt-out, delivery channel specifics, handling of learners who disabled notifications) should be captured separately when the story is groomed.' — this is an allowed brief note that AC will be written later.
